metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

coverage.verify_error: step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: the model hit the token cap before finishing (finish_reason=length) — its output is TRUNCATED; raise max_tokens or split the step

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the conflict between AC4 ('hypothesis suite includes the four named properties') and the Implementation contract ('stdlib random, NOT hypothesis') by updating AC4 to read 'seeded-random property suite includes the four named properties' (or equivalent) so the formal AC matches the decided approach.
findings.finding: AC4 says 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties' but the Implementation contract explicitly says 'Property tests use stdlib random (seeded), NOT hypothesis — the repo declares no hypothesis dep'. An executor reading the formal ACs will treat the AC as the gate; the contradicting contract note is in a different section. This is a direct conflicting signal (E2 signal #3) that is also material to F1 because the AC cannot be satisfied by both approaches simultaneously.
findings.location: AC line 4 ('hypothesis suite includes the four named properties') vs. Implementation contract section ('Property tests use stdlib random (seeded), NOT hypothesis')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties' in AC4 with 'seeded-random (stdlib) property suite includes the four named properties: convergence, no-orphan, conservation, direction-preservation'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align the Tests section prose and AC item 4 with the Implementation Contract decision: replace every occurrence of 'hypothesis' / 'hypothesis suite' with 'seeded-random property tests (stdlib `random`)' so all three sections name the same tool.
findings.finding: The Tests section says 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties' but the Implementation Contract explicitly says 'Property tests use stdlib `random` (seeded), NOT `hypothesis`' — the AC then repeats the word 'hypothesis suite', sending an implementer in two directions about which test infrastructure to use.
findings.location: Tests section (test_properties.py bullet) vs AC item 4 vs Implementation Contract ('Property tests use stdlib `random`')
findings.suggested_fix: In the Tests section, rename the file bullet to 'test_properties.py — seeded-random model-world properties: (a) convergence … (d) direction-preservation'. In AC item 4, replace 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties' with 'seeded-random property suite (stdlib `random`) includes the four named properties (convergence, no-orphan, conservation, direction-preservation)'. Remove the word 'hypothesis' everywhere it refers to this test file.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rewrite AC item 4's frozen-census assertion to distinguish the two counts explicitly: '…asserting EXACTLY 7 decisions total (5x TERMINAL_TRANSITION + 1x PROBE_GET + 1x ADOPT) and `census(...).acting_count == 6` (PROBE_GET is not acting per the circuit-breaker definition).'
findings.finding: AC item 4 says 'asserting EXACTLY 7 acting decisions' for the frozen-census fixture test, but the Implementation Contract defines PROBE_GET as NOT acting and specifies `census(...).acting_count == 6`; the word 'acting' in the AC is inconsistent with the circuit-breaker's definition of 'acting' established in the Circuit Breaker section.
findings.location: AC item 4 (frozen-census assertion) vs Circuit Breaker section ('acting kinds = {TERMINAL_TRANSITION, RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE, ADOPT}') vs Implementation Contract ('the test asserts those 7 decisions by key AND asserts `census(...).acting_count == 6`')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'asserting EXACTLY 7 acting decisions' with 'asserting 7 total decisions (5x TERMINAL_TRANSITION REB-464/456/465/466/457, 1x PROBE_GET REB-530, 1x ADOPT REB-532) and census acting_count == 6 (PROBE_GET excluded from acting per breaker definition)' in AC item 4.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `reconciler.baseline_dual_write: bool = False` to AC item 3 alongside `max_acting_fraction` so it has a covering acceptance criterion.
findings.finding: The Implementation Contract introduces a new config key `reconciler.baseline_dual_write: bool = False` as a deliverable to be registered in `_config_schema.py`, but no Acceptance Criterion covers it — AC item 3 only names `reconciler.max_acting_fraction`.
findings.location: AC item 3 vs Implementation Contract ('A `reconciler.baseline_dual_write: bool = False` key is added alongside')
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC item 3 to: '…config keys `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` (default 0.10) and `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` (default False) registered in `ReconcilerConfig` in `_config_schema.py`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback note to the Per-binding baseline section stating either (a) old code reads the new file safely because it ignores unknown fields and the version bump is advisory, or (b) a back-out procedure (e.g., run a migration script to strip the `baseline` key) is required before rolling back, with a reference to that procedure.
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges backward-compatible READS of the bumped bindings.json version (old entries without `baseline` degrade gracefully), but provides no explicit rollback path for the reverse direction: if the new code writes the new-format file and a deployment rollback forces old code to read it, there is no stated forward-compatibility guarantee or rollback procedure.
findings.location: Per-binding baseline section ('bump the file `version` field; older entries without baseline are VALID') and AC item 2 ('corrupt store still fails closed; version bumped with back-compat read')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a doc anchor (ADR entry, CLAUDE.md section, or inline schema comment with a doc/adr/ pointer) that specifies the `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` key's semantics, activation/deactivation protocol, and failure mode before it is registered in `_config_schema.py`.
findings.finding: `reconciler.baseline_dual_write: bool = False` is introduced as a new operational config key governing a shadow/dual-write phase, but no ADR, design-doc update, or CLAUDE.md entry documents its semantics, activation conditions, or failure modes.
findings.location: Implementation contract — resolved specifics: '`reconciler.baseline_dual_write: bool = False` key is added alongside for the rollout's shadow phase'
findings.suggested_fix: Either extend ADR 0026 (baseline lifecycle) with a 'Dual-write shadow phase' section, or open a stub ADR 0030 that specifies: (1) what dual-write writes, (2) ordering invariants, (3) the condition under which the flag is flipped to True and then removed. Reference it from the _config_schema.py field's docstring.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Register `reconciler.grace_absent_404: int = 3` in `_config_schema.py` alongside `max_acting_fraction`, and document the expected caller pattern (read from config, pass as `grace=` kwarg) so the live-wiring ticket threads it correctly.
findings.finding: The `grace: int = 3` threshold (404-miss count before RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE) is a hardcoded keyword-argument default in `classify()` with no mention of registration in `_config_schema.py`, leaving it effectively untunable in production without a code change.
findings.location: Implementation contract — resolved specifics: 'classify(local, jira, binding, baseline, *, grace: int = 3)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `grace_absent_404: int = 3` to `ReconcilerConfig` in `_config_schema.py` with a validator `_as_int(v, k, minimum=1, maximum=30)`. In the live-wiring ticket's call site, pass `grace=config.reconciler.grace_absent_404`. The classify() signature needs no change.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the invocation path for `seed_baselines_from_snapshot`: define whether it is a CLI subcommand, a migration script run once before the rollout task, or a reconciler startup hook — and add an AC asserting it has been executed before the first live pass that reads `get_baseline`.
findings.finding: The invocation path for seed_baselines_from_snapshot is never specified: the plan names it a 'one-shot seed helper' but does not say who calls it, when (migration script, reconciler startup hook, manual CLI command), or how its completion is verified before the first live pass consumes the baseline state it creates.
findings.location: Per-binding baseline section — 'One-shot seed helper seed_baselines_from_snapshot'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line migration entry-point (e.g., `rebar migrate seed-baselines --snapshot <path>`) and document it as a prerequisite step in the rollout task; the frozen-census test can assert that after running seed on the frozen snapshot all 613 bound keys have a non-None baseline.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Audit or document that the existing `save()` method is atomic (write-to-temp + os.replace or equivalent) and add this as an explicit invariant in the `BindingStore` docstring; if it is not atomic, make it so before set_baseline lands.
findings.finding: The plan adds set_baseline() writing to bindings.json but does not specify whether the write is atomic with respect to concurrent reconciler processes; the existing save() path may not use a write-to-temp-then-rename idiom, leaving the file vulnerable to partial-write corruption if two processes call save() concurrently after independent set_baseline calls.
findings.location: Per-binding baseline section — 'New methods get_baseline / set_baseline; persisted by the existing save()'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` from `ReconcilerConfig` / `_config_schema.py` in this ticket and add it only in the rollout task that actually reads it (confirm with the rollout task owner that deferring the key registration is acceptable).
findings.finding: `reconciler.baseline_dual_write: bool = False` is added to `ReconcilerConfig` in this ticket, but has zero consumers anywhere in this ticket's scope — the plan explicitly says it is 'for the rollout's shadow phase' which is a different, future ticket. Registering an operator-visible config key before any code reads it is premature config-surface proliferation.
findings.location: Implementation contract — resolved specifics / `_config_schema.py` / ReconcilerConfig
findings.suggested_fix: Delete the `baseline_dual_write: bool = False` field from `ReconcilerConfig` and its `_SECTIONS['reconciler']` coercer entry. Add a TODO comment in the rollout task's plan pointing to where it must be registered.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rewrite the Tests-section AC for `test_properties.py` to remove the word 'hypothesis' and replace with 'seeded-random model-world' consistent with the Implementation Contract, so both sections agree and no executor adds the `hypothesis` dep.
findings.finding: The Tests section AC still says 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties', but the Implementation Contract explicitly resolves this away: 'Property tests use stdlib `random` (seeded), NOT `hypothesis` — the repo declares no hypothesis dep and no test uses it.' The contradictory AC text risks a mechanical executor adding the `hypothesis` dep and writing Hypothesis-style tests, which would break CI (zero existing test uses, not in pyproject.toml `[dev]`).
findings.location: Tests section AC / Implementation contract — 'Property tests use stdlib random'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'hypothesis: (a) convergence … (d) direction-preservation' to 'seeded-random model-world (stdlib `random`, seed fixed): (a) convergence … (d) direction-preservation' in the Tests section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extract the grace-threshold predicate into a single named helper (e.g. `_meets_grace(count: int, grace: int) -> bool`) shared by both `classify.py` and `binding_store.note_absent`, or document explicitly in both sites why the +1 offset is intentional and add a cross-reference comment.
findings.finding: The `classify()` function's CONFIRMED_404 → RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE predicate (`binding['absent_404_count'] + 1 >= grace`) duplicates the threshold comparison already implemented in `binding_store.note_absent` (`entry['absent_404_count'] >= grace`, line 311), creating two copies of the same domain logic that must be kept in sync if the grace semantics ever change.
findings.location: New module: classify.py / Implementation contract — Grace-met for RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit ADRs 0026–0029 to `docs/adr/` on this branch (or specify the exact branch/SHA where they reside) before implementation begins, since the plan designates them as the primary spec for all matrix cells, baseline decision points, and ObservedJira state semantics.
findings.finding: ADRs 0026, 0027, 0028, and 0029 are cited as the authoritative spec throughout the plan ('read them FIRST', 'ADR 0026 — read its 4 decision points verbatim', 'four-way observation state of ADR 0028', etc.), but docs/adr/ only contains ADRs 0001–0025; ADRs 0026–0029 do not exist in the repository.
findings.location: Summary section; Per-binding baseline section; New module section (ObservedJira / matrix anchor cells)
findings.suggested_fix: Add the four ADR files to docs/adr/ as part of this ticket's deliverables, or add a plan note: 'ADRs 0026-0029 are on branch X, SHA Y — check them out before reading this plan.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct AC line 4 to read 'seeded-random property suite (stdlib only, no hypothesis dependency) includes the four named properties' so the AC and Implementation Contract are consistent.
findings.finding: The Acceptance Criteria say 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties' but the Implementation Contract section explicitly states 'Property tests use stdlib random (seeded), NOT hypothesis — the repo declares no hypothesis dep and no test uses it'. These two statements directly contradict each other; an executor following AC literally would add a hypothesis dependency that the contract explicitly forbids.
findings.location: Tests section (test_properties.py description); AC line 4 ('hypothesis suite includes the four named properties'); Implementation contract bullet 'Property tests use stdlib random (seeded), NOT hypothesis'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'hypothesis suite' with 'seeded-random property suite (stdlib `random`, no `hypothesis` dep)' in the AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Generate and check in the sanitized frozen fixtures at `tests/fixtures/bridge_convergence/frozen-2026-07-03/` (bindings.json, snapshot.json, locals.json) as part of this ticket's deliverables, since the directory does not currently exist and test_frozen_census.py depends on it.
findings.finding: The frozen-census fixture directory `tests/fixtures/bridge_convergence/frozen-2026-07-03/` (holding bindings.json, snapshot.json, locals.json) is described as already existing in the Implementation Contract ('Frozen fixture provenance') and the AC says 'frozen-census fixture checked in', but the actual `tests/fixtures/` directory contains only `jira/` and `review_bot_merge/` — the `bridge_convergence/` subtree does not exist.
findings.location: Tests section (test_frozen_census.py); Implementation contract 'Frozen fixture provenance'; AC line 4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document or link the 'derisk X4' spike artefact (script, output, or fixture) in the repo so that the '613 bound+present keys all carry all 5 baseline fields' claim is verifiable, or add a defensive guard in `seed_baselines_from_snapshot` that skips/warns on entries with missing fields.
findings.finding: The plan claims 'derisk X4 proved all 613 bound+present keys carry all 5 baseline fields' as the justification for the seed_baselines_from_snapshot helper's correctness, but no spike artefact, fixture, or script verifying this claim exists in the repository — the assertion is unverifiable from the codebase.
findings.location: Per-binding baseline section: 'One-shot seed helper seed_baselines_from_snapshot(prev_snapshot: dict)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a guard: `if not all(k in snapshot_fields for k in ('summary','description','priority','status','assignee')): log_warning_and_skip(local_id)` inside seed_baselines_from_snapshot.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan (or census() docstring) whether the SyncLogger call is `log('decision_census', **census)` (flat top-level keys) or `log('decision_census', census=census)` (nested under 'census' key), and ensure the unit test's expected record shape matches the actual calling convention.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'emitted via the existing SyncLogger.log("decision_census", **census)' where census is the dict `{counts: {kind->int}, acting_count, acting_pct, total, breaker: {...}}`. The real SyncLogger.log() merges **kwargs directly into the flat JSON entry. Passing `**census` will unpack top-level keys including `counts` (a dict) and `breaker` (a dict) as flat kwargs — the resulting JSON line will contain nested dicts at the top level, not a nested `census` object. This is functional but differs from the documented record shape which shows `breaker` as a sub-object under a `census` key.
findings.location: Decision-census telemetry section; Implementation contract 'decision_census record shape'
findings.suggested_fix: Use `sync_logger.log('decision_census', **census)` and update the documented record shape to show the fields are top-level (not nested under 'census'), or use `sync_logger.log('decision_census', census=census)` and update all consumers.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Commit ADRs 0026–0029 under `docs/adr/` on this branch (or explicitly note in the plan that this ticket co-creates them) so 'read them FIRST' resolves to real files before any implementation begins.
findings.finding: ADRs 0026, 0027, 0028, and 0029 are cited as the governing specification ('read them FIRST') but docs/adr/ only contains ADRs 0001–0025; those four files do not exist in the repository.
findings.location: Summary section: 'All invariants are specified by ADRs 0026-0029 (docs/adr/) — read them FIRST'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a subtask/AC entry: 'Create docs/adr/0026-baseline-strategy.md, 0027-*.md, 0028-observation-states.md, 0029-*.md capturing the decisions now inlined in the Implementation Contract section' — the contract text already has the content; formalising it as ADRs satisfies the stated prerequisite.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/fixtures/bridge_convergence/frozen-2026-07-03/{bindings.json,snapshot.json,locals.json}` as explicit CREATION deliverables in the plan (with the PII-stripped generation procedure documented or a generation script referenced).
findings.finding: The fixture directory `tests/fixtures/bridge_convergence/frozen-2026-07-03/` (bindings.json + snapshot.json + locals.json) referenced by the frozen-census AC does not exist in the repository and is not listed as a CREATION target.
findings.location: Tests section: 'tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/classify/test_frozen_census.py — replay sanitized frozen fixtures (tests/fixtures/bridge_convergence/frozen-2026-07-03/…)'; AC bullet 4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly name `_SECTIONS['reconciler']` in `src/rebar/_config_schema.py` as a required co-edit target alongside `ReconcilerConfig`, adding lambda coercers for both `max_acting_fraction` (`_as_float(v, k, minimum=0.0, maximum=1.0)`) and `baseline_dual_write` (`_as_bool(v, k)`).
findings.finding: Adding `max_acting_fraction: float = 0.10` and `baseline_dual_write: bool = False` to `ReconcilerConfig` requires a PARALLEL edit to `_SECTIONS['reconciler']` coercer dict in `_config_schema.py`, but only the dataclass field addition is named; the coercer entries are an unenumerated edit target in the same file.
findings.location: Implementation contract section: 'reconciler.max_acting_fraction — registered in _config_schema.py ReconcilerConfig as float = 0.10, coercer _as_float(v, k, minimum=0.0, maximum=1.0). A reconciler.baseline_dual_write: bool = False key is added alongside'
findings.suggested_fix: In _config_schema.py, add to _SECTIONS['reconciler']: `'max_acting_fraction': lambda v, k: _as_float(v, k, minimum=0.0, maximum=1.0), 'baseline_dual_write': lambda v, k: _as_bool(v, k)` alongside the ReconcilerConfig dataclass fields.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the caller attribution in the Implementation Contract: the sentinel-to-ObservedJira mapping must be wired at the `outbound_differ._safe_get_issue` callsite (outbound_differ.py lines 596–610), not in inbound_probe.py.
findings.finding: The plan attributes `_safe_get_issue` to `inbound_probe` ('inbound_probe/_safe_get_issue sentinels → CONFIRMED_404/TRANSPORT_ERROR') but `_safe_get_issue`, `_DELETED`, and `_TRANSPORT_ERROR` actually live in `outbound_differ.py`; `inbound_probe.py` is a separate module with no such function.
findings.location: Implementation contract section: 'Raw GET outcomes are mapped to ObservedJira by the caller (the live probe path; inbound_probe/_safe_get_issue sentinels → CONFIRMED_404/TRANSPORT_ERROR)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'inbound_probe/_safe_get_issue sentinels' with 'outbound_differ._safe_get_issue sentinels (_DELETED → CONFIRMED_404, _TRANSPORT_ERROR → TRANSPORT_ERROR)' in the Implementation Contract section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document explicitly how the `classify(grace=)` value and `binding_store.note_absent`'s `RECONCILER_ABSENT_RETIRE_GRACE` env-var are kept in sync (e.g. both callers read the same typed config key, or a single constant is shared), and enumerate `note_absent` as a callsite that may need updating when the grace source changes.
findings.finding: The existing `binding_store.note_absent()` reads grace from the `RECONCILER_ABSENT_RETIRE_GRACE` environment variable independently of the new `classify(grace=3)` keyword argument; neither the plan nor AC enumerate `note_absent` as a consumer that may need updating or at least coordination documentation to prevent dual grace thresholds.
findings.location: Per-binding baseline section: 'binding_store.note_absent remains the sole ENACTOR of the increment+retire (classify predicts, note_absent enacts — no double count)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly enumerate and guard the `unbound + CONFIRMED_404` matrix cell in `classify()`: either add it as a named cell (likely `NOOP` — no binding to retire, no action) or add a pre-condition guard that short-circuits before reading `binding['absent_404_count']` when `binding is None`.
findings.finding: The grace-count prediction `binding['absent_404_count'] + 1 >= grace` inside classify() will raise TypeError/KeyError when `binding is None`, which is a valid input for the unbound+CONFIRMED_404 cell (an unbound key whose JiraObservation.state is CONFIRMED_404). The matrix section lists `bound+*+CONFIRMED_404` cells but does not enumerate or guard the unbound+CONFIRMED_404 cell, leaving a crash path that an adversarial or misconfigured caller can hit.
findings.location: Implementation contract — resolved specifics: 'Grace-met for RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE' + Matrix anchor cells section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a guard at the top of the CONFIRMED_404 branch: `if jira.state is ObservedJira.CONFIRMED_404 and binding is None: return Decision(kind=DecisionKind.NOOP, reason='confirmed_404_unbound', payload={})` before the grace-count arithmetic.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly require that BOTH `ReconcilerConfig` dataclass fields AND their corresponding `_SECTIONS['reconciler']` coercer lambda entries are added for `max_acting_fraction` and `baseline_dual_write`, and add a test to `test_config.py` asserting that a TOML value for each key is correctly parsed (not warned-unknown).
findings.finding: The plan states `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` and `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` will be registered in `_config_schema.py`'s `ReconcilerConfig`, but does not specify that the `_SECTIONS['reconciler']` coercer table must also be updated. The existing `ReconcilerConfig` dataclass (4 fields: `jira_cli_timeout`, `lock_max_retries`, `deletion_probe_limit`, `id_guard_bypass_unsafe`) and the parallel `_SECTIONS['reconciler']` dict are tightly coupled — adding a field to the dataclass WITHOUT adding its coercer to `_SECTIONS` means the new key is silently ignored on read (treated as unknown, warned, discarded) rather than parsed and validated.
findings.location: AC line: 'config key `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` (default 0.10) registered in the typed config schema (_config_schema.py)'; Implementation contract: 'ReconcilerConfig as float = 0.10, coercer _as_float(v, k, minimum=0.0, maximum=1.0)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the implementation notes, add: 'In `_SECTIONS["reconciler"]`, add: `"max_acting_fraction": lambda v, k: _as_float(v, k, minimum=0.0, maximum=1.0)` and `"baseline_dual_write": lambda v, k: _as_bool(v, k)` alongside the dataclass field additions.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a section to the plan (or the Implementation Contract) explicitly stating whether classify() REPLACES the existing `_DELETED`/`_TRANSPORT_ERROR` sentinel classification in `outbound_differ._safe_get_issue`, or coexists with it — and if coexisting, specify which takes precedence during the transition period to prevent double-classification.
findings.finding: The novel classify() matrix layer partially duplicates the existing LocalState × ObservedJira classification already embedded in outbound_differ.py (_DELETED/_TRANSPORT_ERROR sentinels, `_safe_get_issue`, `_note_absent`). The plan does not establish whether classify() REPLACES or COEXISTS with this live sentinel logic, nor justify the new matrix layer over refactoring/extending the existing sentinel pattern. A coexistence approach risks double-classification (two conflicting answers for the same bound-absent key on the same pass).
findings.location: New module: src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/classify.py (pure leaf); also outbound_differ.py lines 82–138 (_DELETED/_TRANSPORT_ERROR sentinels)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update AC item 4 to replace 'hypothesis suite' with 'seeded-random property suite' (matching the Implementation Contract resolution) so the AC and the resolved specifics are consistent.
findings.finding: The Acceptance Criteria section (AC line 4) still reads 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties', but the Implementation Contract corrects this to seeded stdlib random. This uncorrected contradiction in the AC will cause the AC to fail if interpreted literally, and it signals the plan text was not fully reconciled after the prior-review remediation.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC item 4: 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties'; Implementation Contract — 'Property tests use stdlib random (seeded), NOT hypothesis'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties' → 'seeded-random property suite (stdlib random, seeded) covers the four named properties'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) specify how `check_blast_radius` reads `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` from within the subprocess (e.g., via an env-var adapter `RECONCILER_MAX_ACTING_FRACTION` matching the existing `_env_int` pattern), or (b) change the plan to register it as an env var (not a ReconcilerConfig field) consistent with every other reconciler tunable (`RECONCILER_ABSENT_RETIRE_GRACE`, `RECONCILER_ABSENT_GET_BUDGET`).
findings.finding: The plan registers `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` and `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` in `ReconcilerConfig` (_config_schema.py), but the reconciler subprocess does not read from ReconcilerConfig — it reads tunables from environment variables via `_env_int` (binding_store.py:49-66, outbound_differ.py:92-104). There is no existing path by which the reconciler subprocess consumes typed ReconcilerConfig fields. Adding fields to ReconcilerConfig that the reconciler never reads provides false assurance; the actual circuit-breaker threshold will be inaccessible unless a new config-read path is also specified.
findings.location: Circuit breaker section ('config key reconciler.max_acting_fraction default 0.10 registered in the typed config schema (_config_schema.py)'); AC item 3; Implementation Contract ('registered in _config_schema.py ReconcilerConfig as float = 0.10')
findings.suggested_fix: Follow the existing pattern: rename to `RECONCILER_MAX_ACTING_FRACTION` env var (parsed defensively by `_env_int(..., minimum=0, maximum=100)` with integer basis-points, or a new `_env_float`), and drop the ReconcilerConfig field registration (or keep it as documentation-only, clearly labeled as such).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the rollback procedure for the `bindings.json` version bump: either (a) add a version-gate in `_load()` that warns/errors when `version > CURRENT_VERSION` (fail-closed on unknown future versions), or (b) document that the `baseline`/`baseline_advanced_at` fields are safely ignored by v1 readers and that the rollback path is a no-op (old code ignores new fields).
findings.finding: The plan bumps the bindings.json `version` field but specifies no rollback procedure and no forward-compatibility guard for old code reading a higher-version file. If the baseline feature is reverted (old code deployed against a version-2 store), `_load()` in binding_store.py has no version-gating logic and will silently read unknown `baseline`/`baseline_advanced_at` fields — which is safe for reads but creates an undefined behavior contract. No procedure to strip those fields (downgrade the file to v1) is described.
findings.location: Per-binding baseline section — 'bump the file `version` field'; AC line 'version bumped with back-compat read'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `STORE_VERSION = 2` constant; in `_load()` after parsing, if `data.get('version', 1) > STORE_VERSION`: raise ValueError with recovery instructions. Document that v1 readers silently ignore unknown fields (Python `dict.get` default) so rollback is safe without a strip step — then remove the rollback-procedure gap by stating this explicitly in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the concrete invocation path for `seed_baselines_from_snapshot` — e.g., a one-time operator CLI subcommand (`rebar reconciler seed-baselines <snapshot-path>`), a pass-startup guard that detects version<2 and auto-seeds from the latest snapshot, or a migration script — so there is no gap between shipping the code and the first classifying pass using baselines.
findings.finding: The plan never specifies WHEN or HOW `seed_baselines_from_snapshot` is invoked in production, creating an unaddressed dual-write timing window: bindings confirmed BETWEEN the `prev_snapshot` capture time and the seed call will receive no baseline (acceptable via local-wins), but the plan gives no operator runbook or automated trigger for the one-shot seed, leaving it unclear whether it runs at pass-start, via a CLI subcommand, or manually. Without a defined invocation point, there is no guarantee the seed runs before the first classifying pass.
findings.location: Per-binding baseline section — 'One-shot seed helper `seed_baselines_from_snapshot(prev_snapshot: dict)`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that ADRs 0026–0029 are checked in under `docs/adr/` on the feature branch before work begins; if they exist only in a spike branch or external doc, move them to `docs/adr/` and commit them as a prerequisite so executors and reviewers can verify matrix cells against the governing spec.
findings.finding: ADRs 0026–0029, cited as the authoritative governing specification throughout the plan ('read them FIRST', 'ADR 0026 — read its 4 decision points verbatim', 'four-way observation state of ADR 0028'), do not exist in docs/adr/ in the current repository (which stops at ADR-0025). The plan asserts they are 'committed under docs/adr/ on this branch', but an executor starting from the current codebase cannot locate them.
findings.location: Summary section ('All invariants are specified by ADRs 0026-0029 (docs/adr/) — read them FIRST') and Implementation contract ('ADRs 0026–0029 are committed under docs/adr/ on this branch')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify that this ticket ADDS `max_acting_fraction: float = 0.10` and `baseline_dual_write: bool = False` to both `ReconcilerConfig` (the dataclass) AND the `_SECTIONS['reconciler']` coercer dict in `_config_schema.py` — neither currently exists (tool-verified).
findings.finding: `reconciler.max_acting_fraction` and `reconciler.baseline_dual_write` are described as already-registered config keys in `_config_schema.py ReconcilerConfig`, but tool-verified inspection shows `ReconcilerConfig` has only 4 existing fields and the `_SECTIONS['reconciler']` coercer table has only those same 4 keys — neither new key exists anywhere in the codebase.
findings.location: Implementation contract section ('reconciler.max_acting_fraction registered in _config_schema.py') + AC line 3 ('config key registered in the typed config schema (_config_schema.py)')
findings.suggested_fix: In the Implementation contract, change 'registered in _config_schema.py ReconcilerConfig' to 'ADD to ReconcilerConfig dataclass: `max_acting_fraction: float = 0.10` and `baseline_dual_write: bool = False`; AND add corresponding lambda coercers to `_SECTIONS["reconciler"]`: `"max_acting_fraction": lambda v, k: _as_float(v, k, minimum=0.0, maximum=1.0)` and `"baseline_dual_write": lambda v, k: _as_bool(v, k)`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit ordering contract: specify that the live path MUST call `classify()` FIRST (reads the current count to predict), then — if and only if the decision is `PROBE_GET` or `RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE` — call `note_absent()` to enact the increment/retirement, and that `note_absent()` must NOT be called when classify returns `NOOP` (TRANSPORT_ERROR branch).
findings.finding: The predict/enact split between `classify()` and `note_absent()` has an ambiguous counter-increment placement: `classify()` is specified to predict `RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE` by reading `binding["absent_404_count"] + 1 >= grace`, but the plan does not specify when/whether `note_absent` (the sole enactor of the increment) is called by the live path after a `RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE` decision — leaving it unclear whether `classify` reads a pre-increment count and `note_absent` fires after, or whether a double-increment is possible.
findings.location: Implementation contract section ('Grace-met for RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE') and 'Per-binding baseline' section ('binding_store.note_absent remains the sole ENACTOR')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC4 with the Implementation contract by replacing 'hypothesis suite' with 'seeded-random property suite' (or equivalent unambiguous wording) so the acceptance criterion matches the specified technology.
findings.finding: AC4 says 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties' but the Implementation contract explicitly states 'Property tests use stdlib random (seeded), NOT hypothesis — the repo declares no hypothesis dep and no test uses it'. These two statements directly contradict each other within the same plan, and `hypothesis` is confirmed absent from the codebase.
findings.location: AC line 4 ('hypothesis suite includes the four named properties') vs. Implementation contract section ('Property tests use stdlib random (seeded), NOT hypothesis')
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC4 from 'hypothesis suite includes the four named properties' to 'seeded-random (stdlib random, fixed seed) property suite includes the four named properties (convergence, no-orphan, conservation, direction-preservation)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit mapping table to the plan specifying how each `inbound_probe.ProbeBranch` value (and each `_safe_get_issue` sentinel, if used) maps to `ObservedJira` states, co-located with the `JiraObservation` dataclass definition.
findings.finding: The plan says the caller (the 'live probe path') maps raw GET outcomes to `ObservedJira` via '`inbound_probe`/`_safe_get_issue` sentinels', but these are two distinct, incompatible systems: `inbound_probe.py` returns `ProbeBranch` enum values (`PRESENT_RESOLVED`, `ARCHIVED_OR_MOVED`, etc.) while `outbound_differ._safe_get_issue` returns sentinel objects (`_DELETED`, `_TRANSPORT_ERROR`). The plan never specifies which one is the authoritative source for building `JiraObservation`, nor does it specify the mapping table from either system's outputs to `ObservedJira` states.
findings.location: Implementation contract section ('Grace-met for RETIRE_AFTER_GRACE'): 'Raw GET outcomes are mapped to ObservedJira by the caller (the live probe path; inbound_probe/_safe_get_issue sentinels → CONFIRMED_404/TRANSPORT_ERROR)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Implementation contract: 'The JiraObservation builder (a new thin adapter, NOT inside classify.py) maps: ProbeBranch.ARCHIVED_OR_MOVED → ObservedJira.CONFIRMED_404; ProbeBranch.UNREACHABLE → ObservedJira.TRANSPORT_ERROR; ProbeBranch.PRESENT_RESOLVED / PRESENT_FILTERED → ObservedJira.PRESENT; snapshot miss (key absent from fetcher window) → ObservedJira.ABSENT_IN_WINDOW. The _safe_get_issue sentinels are NOT used by the new classify path.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
